sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Ford Motor Company is investigating accident that killed employee
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company is investigating an industrial accident at its Sharonville Transmission plant that resulted in the death of employee Gregory Knopf. The incident occurred when a press machine undergoing routine maintenance was accidentally turned on, pinning Knopf. This article also covers other unrelated labor disputes, including potential strikes by Teamsters at INEOS and nurses at Corewell Health East, and a call from the AFL-CIO regarding voter suppression legislation.

feed.title: Posco, Molten to collaborate on graphite production for battery anodes
feed.summary: Posco Future M, a battery materials subsidiary of South Korea’s Posco Holdings, has partnered with US start-up Molten Industries Inc to produce graphite for battery anodes using methane gas. This collaboration aims to reduce reliance on mined graphite and lower production costs for EV battery anode materials. Molten Industries will use its pyrolysis process to convert methane into hydrogen and graphite, which Posco's Future Graph will then process into spherical forms for anodes.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks Taps Danielle Gonzalez to Lead Talent Strategy as Chief People Officer
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has appointed Danielle Gonzalez as its new Chief People Officer. In this role, Gonzalez will be responsible for modernizing the company's talent engine and fostering a high-performance culture as the cybersecurity operations expand. She brings over 20 years of HR leadership experience and previously served as SVP of the people team for go-to-market and next-generation security businesses at Palo Alto Networks.

feed.title: Gold Pullback Creates Opportunity: Pan American Silver, Kobo Resources, and Agnico Eagle in Focus
feed.summary: The article discusses the recent pullback in gold prices, presenting it as an investment opportunity for gold stocks. It highlights three companies: Agnico Eagle Mines for its strong cost management and operational security, Kobo Resources as a growth stock with high potential in West Africa, and Pan American Silver for its exposure to both silver and gold. The author advises a balanced portfolio including both established producers and high-growth exploration companies.

feed.title: Waste Management, Inc. (NYSE:WM) Receives Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts

Insider selling has occurred, but institutional investors have significantly increased their holdings, and the company recently raised its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: A Look At Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) Valuation After US$1.1b Luxury Resort Sales
feed.summary: Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) recently sold two luxury resorts for a combined US$1.1 billion, a move aimed at increasing financial flexibility. The stock is currently trading at US$19.10, with analysts suggesting it is moderately undervalued by around 5.3% due to prudent balance sheet management. However, its P/E ratio of 17.2x is higher than the global Hotel and Resort REITs average.

feed.title: Will Viatris’ 2030 Capital and R&D Framework Reset the Core Investment Narrative for VTRS?
feed.summary: Viatris Inc. recently outlined its long-term vision, growth priorities, R&D focus, and financial framework through 2030 at an Investor Event. The company plans to use anticipated product launches, a developing pipeline, business development, and cash flow to fund both reinvestment and shareholder returns, maintaining its 2026 dividend policy despite recent losses. This framework aims to reshape Viatris's investment narrative, moving past its generics-heavy portfolio toward sustainable cash generation by 2030, though execution on new launches and ongoing pricing pressures remain key challenges.

feed.title: Trump Says Lockheed Martin, Raytheon Are Building Like 'Never Seen Before,' Defends $200 Billion Pentagon Request
feed.summary: Former President Donald Trump has defended a proposed $200 billion increase in the Pentagon's budget, citing depleted ammunition stockpiles due to aid to Ukraine and significant production efforts by defense contractors like Lockheed Martin and Raytheon. He stated that these companies are building at unprecedented levels following pressure from his administration, which also prevented massive stock buybacks. Critics, including Democrats Hakeem Jeffries and Gavin Newsom, argue against the defense spending, suggesting the funds could better address domestic issues like healthcare and education.

feed.title: PAR SEC Filings - Par Technology 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K Forms
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive resource for Par Technology (PAR) SEC filings, including 10-K, 10-Q, and 8-K reports, along with insider trading forms. It details the company's financial structure, revenue mix, and significant events, offering insights into its subscription services and acquisition activities. Stock Titan enhances this information with AI-powered tools for summarizing filings and providing real-time updates.

feed.title: One Stop Systems (OSS) Swings To Q3 Profit Challenging Longstanding Loss Narrative
feed.summary: One Stop Systems (OSS) reported a mixed Q3 2025, swinging to a small profit of US$0.3 million after five quarters of losses, with revenue reaching US$18.8 million. Despite this recent profitability, the company still faces a trailing 12-month loss of approximately US$6.9 million, indicating that long-term profitability is not yet established. Analysts and investors are closely watching whether the positive margin trends can be sustained given the company's past volatility and reliance on lumpy government contracts.

feed.title: Florida Chipotle Mexican Grill coming to Martin County. See where
feed.summary: A new Chipotle Mexican Grill is set to open in the Martin Downs Village Center shopping plaza in Palm City, marking the first for Palm City and third for Martin County. This development was unanimously approved by the Martin County Commission in April 2025. Additionally, a second Chipotle is planned for Fort Pierce.

feed.title: Fort Pierce leaders want to 'dump' Waste Management
feed.summary: Fort Pierce city leaders are expressing their desire to terminate their contract with Waste Management Inc. This decision comes after more than two years of Waste Management transporting thousands of tons of trash daily from Miami-Dade County to a railway yard near Downtown Fort Pierce. Commissioners no longer want to collaborate with the company for the disposal of Fort Pierce's own trash at Waste Management's Okeechobee Landfill.

feed.title: Owens Corning Residential Insulation: Innovations Driving Energy Efficiency in 2026 Housing Boom
feed.summary: Owens Corning has launched enhanced residential insulation lines, including PINK Fiberglas with higher R-values and moisture resistance, to meet the EU's tightened 2026 energy efficiency mandates. These innovations position Owens Corning to gain significant market share in the DACH region, where increasing energy costs and government grants are fueling a €12 billion retrofit market. The new products offer superior thermal performance, fire safety, and sustainability, leading to strong investor interest.

feed.title: Insider Buying: Insperity (NYSE:NSP) CEO Acquires 25,000 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Insperity CEO Paul Sarvadi purchased 25,000 shares of the company's stock for $590,000 on March 19, increasing his total holdings to 997,912 shares. This insider buying occurred despite the company missing quarterly earnings expectations, reporting a negative EPS, and having a high dividend yield with a negative payout ratio. Analysts currently have an average "Reduce" rating on the stock with an average target price of $47.50.

feed.title: 2,216 Shares in Microsoft Corporation $MSFT Acquired by KERR FINANCIAL PLANNING Corp

feed.title: 14,596 Shares in Apple Inc. $AAPL Acquired by KERR FINANCIAL PLANNING Corp
feed.summary: KERR FINANCIAL PLANNING Corp has acquired 14,596 shares of Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) during the third quarter, representing a new position valued at approximately $3.93 million and making Apple its fourth-largest holding. Despite positive catalysts, the article notes potential risks such as security alerts, insider selling, declining capital expenditures, and supply-chain shifts towards competitors like Nvidia.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Decreases Position in IDEX Corporation $IEX

Despite this, institutional ownership remains high at 97.96%, with other institutions like Nordea significantly increasing their holdings. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $228.22, following IDEX's strong Q3 earnings and positive FY2026 guidance.

feed.title: Class action targets Costco over tariff refunds to members
feed.summary: A new federal class action lawsuit has been filed against Costco Wholesale Corp. in Puerto Rico, alleging the retailer is improperly retaining over $20 million in tariff refunds that customers paid through elevated prices. The lawsuit claims Costco raised prices on imported goods in 2025 to cover tariffs later struck down, and is now seeking to keep the refunds and interest rather than reimbursing members. Plaintiff Guillermo J. Ortiz is seeking restitution and class certification for all affected Costco members in Puerto Rico.

feed.title: What’s really on your electric bill? CT lawmakers want it fully spelled out
feed.summary: Connecticut lawmakers are pushing for legislation to redesign electric bills to provide more transparency, particularly regarding public benefits charges. Senator Norm Needleman, D-Essex, stated that current bills are misunderstood by consumers, leading to "cumulative misinformation" about rising costs. While some legislators agree on the need for clarity, others express concerns about the cost and effectiveness of redesigning bills again so soon after a previous attempt.

feed.title: Power Integrations, Inc. $POWI Shares Sold by JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. reduced its stake in Power Integrations (NASDAQ:POWI) by 2.2% in the third quarter, selling 37,527 shares and now owning about 1.67 million shares. Despite this, other institutions like Wellington Management Group and Norges Bank significantly increased their positions or opened new stakes. Power Integrations exceeded EPS expectations ($0.23 vs. $0.19 est.) with revenue of $103.2 million and also announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.215, though it reports a high payout ratio of 220.51%.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Has $66.23 Million Holdings in Domino's Pizza Inc $DPZ
